project.name: East Village / Hawksbill Cove
project.summary: East Village / Hawksbill Cove is a proposed major residential subdivision in Hampstead near Highway 17 and Country Club Road. The submitted preliminary plat package describes 485 single-family lots on about 147 acres within a larger 376-acre planned development area. The plans show phased development, private roads, stormwater ponds, wetlands, floodplain areas, and utility infrastructure. The application is associated with Ryan Homes and property owned by Hampstead Properties LLC.
project.locationDescription: Off Country Club Road between Avila Avenue and Velinza Plantation Road, near Highway 17 in Hampstead.
project.latestUpdate: Preliminary construction plan set dated October 3, 2025.
project.nextStep: County technical review and public review process to be verified.

timeline.title: Preliminary plat application signed
timeline.label: Application Signed

timeline.title: Preliminary plan set prepared
timeline.label: Plan Set Dated
timeline.description: The Hawksbill Cove East Village construction plan cover sheet lists an initial plan date of October 3, 2025.

timeline.title: County technical review
timeline.label: Technical Review
timeline.description: The application is a preliminary plat for a major subdivision. The exact Technical Review Committee date and review comments should be verified from county records before publication.

traffic.notes: The extracted pages reviewed here identify the location and road network, but no traffic impact study totals or required improvements were verified in the package text.

graphicsSuggestions.label: Overview
graphicsSuggestions.reason: Cover sheet includes project data, vicinity/overall context, parcel data, and sheet index.

graphicsSuggestions.label: Construction Phasing
graphicsSuggestions.reason: Phasing plan identifies East Village phases A, B, and C and shows the broader Hawksbill Cove context.

graphicsSuggestions.label: Neighborhood Layout
graphicsSuggestions.reason: Site plan pages begin showing individual lots, roads, limits of disturbance, wetlands, and flood limits. User should choose the clearest page in Graphics.

graphicsSuggestions.label: Environmental Features
graphicsSuggestions.reason: The phasing/overall plan labels existing wetlands, flood limits, normal high water line, 75-foot AEC, and limit of moderate wave action.

graphicsSuggestions.label: Stormwater
graphicsSuggestions.reason: The cover sheet index lists storm drainage, grading, and SCM plan/detail sheets. User should select the best stormwater sheet in Graphics.

graphicsSuggestions.label: Utilities
graphicsSuggestions.reason: The cover sheet index lists multiple utility plan sheets and force main plan/profile sheets.

communityConcerns.summary: The project is located near Highway 17 and Country Club Road, with access context involving Country Club Road, Avila Avenue, and Velinza Plantation Road. Traffic impacts and required road improvements should be verified from county or NCDOT records.
Plan notes and sheets identify existing wetlands, FEMA floodplain, normal high water line, 75-foot AEC, and limit of moderate wave action within or near the project area.
The plan set includes storm drainage, grading, and SCM sheets. Specific stormwater design details should be reviewed from those sheets before public summary text is finalized.
The preliminary plat package describes 485 single-family lots on approximately 147 acres within a larger planned development area.

sourceReferences.value: Approximately 164.47 acres shown on cover sheet

sourceReferences.title: Pender County Unified Development Ordinance
sourceReferences.notes: County zoning, subdivision, and land-use rules used to review development applications.

reviewFlags.message: Application page says approximately 147 acres, while the cover sheet lists East Village area as approximately 164.47 acres. Keep 147 acres as the public preliminary plat area unless county staff clarifies otherwise.
Status is set to Pending based on the project context. Verify current status from the Pender County Major Subdivisions list before publishing.
No verified traffic impact totals or required road improvements were extracted from the reviewed pages.
TRC meeting date, staff comments, Planning Board date, and final decision milestones are not verified in this package.
